DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-07-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Defendant in Pro Per Leah Ahn and Attorney Camilo Artiga-Purcell (415)226-7651 for Defendant Ahe Ahn the moving parties.
Attorney Elizabeth Hurwitz of Utrecht & Lenvin, LLP (415)357-0600 for Plaintiff Priya Sanger the opposing party.

After hearing arguments from both parties in the matter of DEFENDANT AHE AHN's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Overruled. Plaintiffs have sufficiently pled facts for the "judicial foreclosure" cause of action, and Plaintiff is not prevented from bringing this action due to the 2012 Judgment. Plaintiffs have sufficiently pled facts for the "partition by sale" cause of action, including the futility of fully obliging with Section 13.3 of the TICA. Plaintiffs have sufficiently pled facts for the "ejectment" cause of action, specifically Plaintiffs allege facts demonstrating Defendant is in wrongful possession of the property. Lastly, Plaintiffs have sufficiently pled facts seeking declaratory relief. Plaintiffs have pled facts to support they have an interest in the relief.
Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).
Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk J. Rumsey, Not Recorded. (501)
